mnemonics: Memory Addresses Are Like PO Box Numbers (Each location gets a unique number for mail/data delivery)
MAR Holds the Address, MDR Holds the Data (Memory Address Register & Memory Data Register work as a pair)
Big Endian: Big Brother (MSB) Goes First. Little Endian: Little Sibling (LSB) Goes First.
keyPoints: A memory address is a unique identifier for a location in memory, much like a street address.
The Memory Address Register (MAR) holds the address of the data to be accessed.
The Memory Data Register (MDR) holds the data that is read from or written to the address in the MAR.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Memory Address Register (MAR) with the Memory Data Register (MDR).
Misunderstanding byte ordering: assuming a system uses little-endian when it is actually big-endian, or vice-versa.
Forgetting that addressability (byte-addressable vs. word-addressable) changes the numerical value of subsequent addresses.
